ansi_z136_1.title: ANSI Z 136.1 - Safe Use of Lasers
ansi_z136_1.scope: User requirements for eyewear selection and use
requirements: Eyewear must be labeled with OD and wavelength
Eyewear must be appropriate for laser parameters

ansi_z87_1.title: ANSI Z87.1 - Occupational Eye and Face Protection

en_207.title: EN 207 - European Standard for Laser Eyewear

scale_numbers.description: EN 207 uses scale numbers (D, I, R, M) with wavelength ranges

en_208.title: EN 208 - European Standard for Alignment Eyewear
en_208.requirement: Allows visible laser light for alignment while providing protection

spectral_transmittance.requirement: Transmission must be below 10^(-OD) at rated wavelength

damage_threshold.test: Exposure to high-power laser to verify damage resistance
damage_threshold.requirement: Eyewear must withstand rated power/energy without degradation
damage_threshold.method: Controlled exposure testing per EN 207/ANSI standards

requirements.basic_impact: Drop ball test (1 inch steel ball from 50 inches)
requirements.high_impact: High mass impact test (500g projectile from 130cm)

checklist: Check for scratches that could affect vision
Check for discoloration or damage to filters

transmission_verification.method: Compare power with and without eyewear

fit_testing.description: Ensure proper fit for each user
checks: No gaps between frame and face
Temples grip securely without pressure points
Does not interfere with other PPE

required_labeling.wavelength_range: Wavelength(s) of protection in nm or μm

certification_markings.placement: Labels must be permanent and clearly visible

documentation_review: Verify OD ratings meet calculated needs
physical_inspection: Verify labeling is complete and legible

failure_criteria.disposal: Damaged eyewear must be destroyed to prevent accidental reuse
